title: The Internet Predator Dad
theme: A town never forgets. It just redecorates.
omen: a pool gate standing open where nobody remembers a gate
relationship_dynamic: affair exposed through an innocent device sync

contradiction_ledger.entry: Tess Hale is called Daniel in one kitchen scene and Derek during the rain argument.
Elias Bennett says Bellweather is in Vermont, then calls it outside Atlanta after two glasses of wine.
A dead best friend appears at brunch and nobody agrees whether this is denial, evil, or scheduling.
The basement belongs to a condo, a lake house, and an abandoned vineyard at different times in Marigold Falls.

dialogue_samples: "You think because you wear flannel and own a dock light I am supposed to trust you, Tess?"
"Mothers do not disappear, Gavin. They evaporate into paperwork."
"I checked his social media likes, Elias. Nobody who enjoys motivational quotes is innocent."
"The vindictive music box is not decorative anymore and you know it."
"Trust is just fear with a casserole dish, Luke."
"You seem upset," he said, making tea in a way that felt prosecutable.
"If that basement is innocent, why does it sound defensive?"
"I did not lie. I just told the truth in the order this town could survive."
"Honey, ever since the accident, your anxiety has been so high. Did you take your pills?"
"That pool was supposed to make us look safe, and somehow it became a witness."
"He keeps calling it stress like stress knows my garage code."
"If she is the nanny, why is she wearing my robe like a legal argument?"
fake_critic_blurb: "The Internet Predator Dad understands that menace is a regional utility." - County Suspense Quarterly
sequel_hook: Channel 8 Night Report hints that the vineyard fire had a witness who still hosts brunch.
synopsis: Inspired by true events in the emotional sense only, "The Internet Predator Dad" follows Elias Bennett, a sacred protagonist caught inside affair exposed through an innocent device sync before she understood where the gap had already opened. Tess Hale collides with her life carrying portable suspicion, plausible access to offsite storage, and the kind of calm that sounds premeditated in a dim kitchen. The governing injury is simple: Dad thinks he is catching predators and is dangerously wrong about something important. The signature tell is always this: The county telegraphs the problem once and nobody listens until act three. As a pool gate standing open where nobody remembers a gate, what she cannot yet see becomes the syntax of the whole county, and every withheld fact keeps breathing just ahead of her. Where every secret comes home.
